summary: Resolve four automated-review findings: collide-free TOML tag for the second auth env var, single required MCP credential, an XML-correct default Accept header, and recover the records that plays/thing --all dropped when it could not unwrap BGG's nested XML-normalized list shape.
reason: The generated CLI exposes two interchangeable bearer env vars (BGG_TOKEN and its BOARDGAMEGEEK_TOKEN alias). The print derived an identical `toml:"token"` tag for both Config fields, so go-toml/v2 last-wins made BggToken unserializable and risked duplicate `token =` keys corrupting a saved config; the MCP manifest also marked both aliases required, forcing the same secret to be entered twice; and the if-empty Accept default was application/json on an XML-only API, so any direct client call (outside the promoted commands that set an application/xml headerOverride) could draw a 406. Separately, paginatedGet's extractPaginatedItems could not unwrap BGG's XML-normalized shape {"plays":{"@total":"500","play":[...]}} — 'plays' is not a known envelope key and its value is an object, not an array — so --all silently returned [] and discarded every record.
validated_outcome: go build ./... and go vet ./... pass. config.go now tags BoardgamegeekToken as toml:"boardgamegeek_token" (distinct from BggToken's toml:"token"), so both round-trip independently and env-var bearer auth is unchanged. manifest.json marks only bgg_token required (boardgamegeek_token is an optional alias). client.go defaults Accept to application/xml for this XML-only API while binary responses still negotiate */*. helpers.go extractPaginatedItems now unwraps a lone XML wrapper object and resolves the repeated element whether it arrives as an array or (single-occurrence) a collapsed object, skipping @-prefixed attribute keys. Verified live against the BGG API: 'plays --username eekspider --all' returns 100 records (previously []), 'thing --id 13 --all' returns its single item with no extra page fetch (no regression on the single-resource endpoint).

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Generator-level dedupe of credential-field TOML tags when two auth env vars share a suffix (e.g. BGG_TOKEN + BOARDGAMEGEEK_TOKEN both reduce to `token` via EnvVarPlaceholder).
deferred_to_upstream.reason: credentialFields() derives each tag from the env-var suffix and does not detect collisions, so any multi-alias bearer auth prints two struct fields with the same toml tag. The fix belongs in cli-printing-press credentialFields(), not per-CLI.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Generator-level XML-aware Accept default (now landed upstream): client.go.tmpl defaults the if-empty Accept header to application/xml when the spec HasXMLResponse.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Carried as a patch here because the published print predates the cli-printing-press change; regenerating against a press that includes the XML Accept default reproduces this byte-for-byte.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: Generator-level XML-aware pagination unwrap: extractPaginatedItems should recognize the BadgerFish-lite nested-list shape ({wrapper:{...,repeated:[...]}}) for any spec with an XML response_format, not just via a per-CLI patch.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Every page-based command on an XML-backed API (plays, guild, thing here) hits the same nested-shape unwrap gap. The fix belongs in cli-printing-press's paginatedGet template so future XML CLIs auto-paginate correctly.

deferred_to_upstream.feature: True multi-page advancement for page-numbered pagination without a hasMore/limit signal. This patch recovers the first page's records (and keeps the existing missing-pagination-signal warning) but does not auto-advance the page param, because the shared paginatedGet cannot distinguish a genuine list endpoint (plays/guild) from a single-resource endpoint (thing) that ignores page — blindly advancing would re-fetch thing up to the 100-page cap.
deferred_to_upstream.reason: Safe page advancement needs an endpoint-level signal (e.g. BGG's @total attribute on the list wrapper, or an explicit list-vs-resource hint) that only the generator can wire per endpoint.
